current_focus: Treat K=128 only as the initial search anchor, not as a success criterion. The research objective is to find configurations whose FVU is dramatically better than the current K=128 baseline, with a target on the order of halving that baseline FVU before rewarding smaller K or lower cost.
architecture_findings: round 1: A JumpReLU encoder with learnable per-latent thresholds can improve the K=128 quality anchor versus plain TopK by suppressing weak activations before top-k selection, improving feature selectivity and lowering FVU without changing the execution layer. -> crash (fvu=None, observed_fvu=None, k=None, health=crash, termination=identical_to_baseline)

round 2: The new JumpReLU family deserves its first proxy measurement at the K=128 quality anchor; learnable thresholds with smooth gating may suppress weak activations and beat plain TopK FVU before any cost-oriented moves. -> keep (fvu=0.1887873373925686, observed_fvu=0.184100, k=128, health=normal, termination=completed)

round 7: A gated SAE that decouples latent support selection from reconstruction magnitude can beat the current JumpReLU anchor at K=128 by learning a separate sigmoid routing branch while staying initialization-close to plain ReLU top-k. -> crash (fvu=None, observed_fvu=None, k=None, health=crash, termination=identical_to_baseline)

round 26: Recent gated LR sweeps regressed, which suggests the quality ceiling is now architectural rather than a simple optimizer bracket. A new routed SAE family that separates top-k selection scores from decoded magnitudes, while warm-starting the support branch from the magnitude encoder, may preserve useful medium-strength features that plain gated currently suppresses too early and thus reduce FVU at K=128. -> crash (fvu=None, observed_fvu=None, k=None, health=crash, termination=identical_to_baseline)

round 30: The routed family did not get a fair read because its initial support selection was too close to baseline top-k and tripped the identical-to-baseline guard. Making routed support depend on an independently initialized router with a direct, non-monotone routing bonus should produce a genuine architecture signal in proxy and reveal whether separate support selection can outperform the stalled gated search. -> crash (fvu=None, observed_fvu=None, k=None, health=crash, termination=identical_to_baseline)

round 32: Severe stagnation and two non-competitive K=128 architecture probes suggest the current global-ranking support mechanisms are locally exhausted. A new `group_topk` prototype that enforces one local winner per small latent group before the global top-k may reduce redundant feature selection and improve reconstruction quality or cost under the same validated training regime. -> crash (fvu=None, observed_fvu=None, k=None, health=crash, termination=invalid_env_overrides)
performance_findings: round 3: suspected performance regression for jumprelu (tps=16601.650675, baseline_ratio=0.200000, termination=throughput_too_low)

failure_patterns.pattern: A JumpReLU encoder with learnable per-latent thresholds can improve the K=128 quality anchor versus plain TopK by suppressing weak activations before top-k selection, improving feature selectivity and lowering FVU without changing the execution layer.

JumpReLU can reduce FVU further at K=128 by making its gating sharper and more selective; increasing the initial threshold while narrowing the bandwidth should suppress weak activations more aggressively and improve reconstruction quality.

A gated SAE that decouples latent support selection from reconstruction magnitude can beat the current JumpReLU anchor at K=128 by learning a separate sigmoid routing branch while staying initialization-close to plain ReLU top-k.

The current JumpReLU search direction appears locally exhausted, and a gated SAE that separates support selection from reconstruction magnitude may unlock a lower-FVU regime at K=128 by learning support with an explicit sigmoid routing branch while keeping the strong baseline optimization settings.

JumpReLU tuning appears fundamentally stalled, so the next credible move is to test the gated SAE family at K=128, where a separate sigmoid routing branch may learn cleaner support patterns than JumpReLU and reduce FVU under the same strong optimization settings.

Recent gated LR sweeps regressed, which suggests the quality ceiling is now architectural rather than a simple optimizer bracket. A new routed SAE family that separates top-k selection scores from decoded magnitudes, while warm-starting the support branch from the magnitude encoder, may preserve useful medium-strength features that plain gated currently suppresses too early and thus reduce FVU at K=128.

The routed family did not get a fair read because its initial support selection was too close to baseline top-k and tripped the identical-to-baseline guard. Making routed support depend on an independently initialized router with a direct, non-monotone routing bonus should produce a genuine architecture signal in proxy and reveal whether separate support selection can outperform the stalled gated search.

Severe stagnation and two non-competitive K=128 architecture probes suggest the current global-ranking support mechanisms are locally exhausted. A new `group_topk` prototype that enforces one local winner per small latent group before the global top-k may reduce redundant feature selection and improve reconstruction quality or cost under the same validated training regime.

After nine non-improving rounds and non-competitive K=128 results from topk and routed, the current global support-ranking direction looks structurally weak. The newly added `group_topk` family is the next fundamentally different architecture probe because it imposes local winner-take-all competition before global selection, which may improve feature diversity and reduce redundant support allocation under the same validated training regime.

jumprelu.design_hypothesis: Returning to the best known working JumpReLU K=128 configuration and modestly increasing AUXK_ALPHA to 0.0625 may improve training stability and feature health without changing the core architecture or optimization regime, potentially recovering some proxy quality after the recent stalled sweeps.
known_issues: round 3: suspected performance regression for jumprelu (tps=16601.650675, baseline_ratio=0.200000, termination=throughput_too_low)
next_steps: If AUXK_ALPHA=0.0625 improves proxy FVU, promote to full and treat the higher-auxk regime as the new stable JumpReLU anchor.
If AUXK_ALPHA=0.0625 is worse but training remains healthy, test LR=2e-4 on the same restored baseline as the next stability-oriented adjustment.
If stability problems persist even on the restored baseline, pause architecture exploration and continue only with conservative recovery steps around the known-good JumpReLU configuration.

gated.design_hypothesis: With the gated family now improved at K=128 via AUXK_ALPHA=0.01, the next clean move is to test whether that stronger regime transfers to a smaller sparsity budget and improves the Pareto frontier rather than only the anchor point. A gated K=64 proxy run under the same stabilized settings can reveal whether the current best family can materially outperform the old JumpReLU K=64 point.
next_steps: If gated K=64 proxy is clearly better than the current JumpReLU K=64 point, promote it to full and consider gated the new mainline family across multiple K values.
If gated K=64 is competitive but not clearly better, test whether a smaller expansion factor improves the low-K cost-quality tradeoff within the gated family.
If gated K=64 is poor, keep gated focused on K=128 quality work and revisit a different architecture family for lower-K exploration.

topk.design_hypothesis: Five non-improving rounds on gated-specific tweaks suggest the current search direction is locally exhausted, so the next meaningful move is to revisit a fundamentally different sparse selection mechanism. A clean top-k mainline comparison at K=128 using the current strongest training regime may show whether gated-specific routing is the bottleneck rather than the optimizer or regularization settings.
next_steps: If top-k proxy FVU improves materially over its earlier result and is competitive with gated, promote it to full and begin top-k stabilization from this stronger anchor.
If top-k remains clearly worse even under the stronger regime, conclude that a different architectural direction is needed and retire top-k from near-term focus.
If top-k is competitive on quality but notably better on runtime or stability, keep it alive as an alternate mainline family for subsequent cost-quality tradeoff work.

routed.design_hypothesis: Seven non-improving rounds indicate the current gated-centered search is fundamentally stalled, and the updated routed encoder is now the highest-value different architecture to test because it separates support selection from reconstruction magnitude without relying on the same gated monotone ranking. A clean proxy run of routed at K=128 under the best validated training regime can reveal whether this support-selection change opens a new quality path that top-k and gated did not.
next_steps: If routed proxy FVU is competitive with or better than top-k and gated, keep the family alive and promote or stabilize it as the next main architecture path.
If routed runs cleanly but is clearly worse, conclude that this support-selection variant is not the missing ingredient and deprioritize it.
If routed quality is promising but throughput regresses, follow with a routed-specific performance pass rather than discarding the family on speed alone.

group_topk.design_hypothesis: After nine non-improving rounds and non-competitive K=128 results from topk and routed, the current global support-ranking direction looks structurally weak. The newly added `group_topk` family is the next fundamentally different architecture probe because it imposes local winner-take-all competition before global selection, which may improve feature diversity and reduce redundant support allocation under the same validated training regime.
next_steps: If `group_topk` proxy FVU is competitive with or better than topk and routed, keep the family alive and stabilize `group_topk_size` as the next family-specific axis.
If `group_topk` is only modest on quality but shows better runtime or memory behavior, evaluate it as a Pareto-tradeoff family rather than discarding it immediately.
If `group_topk` is clearly worse on both quality and cost signals, retire this local-competition idea and move to a more expressive multi-branch or factorized encoder family.

recent_rounds.hypothesis: The new gated K=128 frontier at EXPANSION_FACTOR=16 may still be under-optimized at LR=1.6e-3, and increasing the signum learning rate to 3.2e-3 could improve reconstruction further now that the higher-capacity gated encoder has shown clear full-run gains.

The gated K=128 EF=16 model appears sensitive to optimization, and the worse full result at LR=3.2e-3 suggests overshooting; reducing the signum learning rate to 8e-4 may improve full-run reconstruction by trading some proxy speed for a stabler optimization trajectory.

Recent gated LR sweeps regressed, which suggests the quality ceiling is now architectural rather than a simple optimizer bracket. A new routed SAE family that separates top-k selection scores from decoded magnitudes, while warm-starting the support branch from the magnitude encoder, may preserve useful medium-strength features that plain gated currently suppresses too early and thus reduce FVU at K=128.

The gated K=128 EF=16 model has now regressed at both 3.2e-3 and 8e-4, so the remaining clean learning-rate check is whether a more conservative signum step size of 4e-4 recovers a smoother optimization path and improves proxy FVU relative to the failed lower-LR probe.

With the gated K=128 EF=16 architecture now worse across all tested learning-rate alternatives, the next clean stabilization axis is auxiliary support pressure: increasing AUXK_ALPHA from 0.03125 to 0.0625 may improve feature health and residual coverage enough to recover proxy FVU without changing the core architecture or optimization regime.

Five non-improving rounds on gated-specific tweaks suggest the current search direction is locally exhausted, so the next meaningful move is to revisit a fundamentally different sparse selection mechanism. A clean top-k mainline comparison at K=128 using the current strongest training regime may show whether gated-specific routing is the bottleneck rather than the optimizer or regularization settings.

The routed family did not get a fair read because its initial support selection was too close to baseline top-k and tripped the identical-to-baseline guard. Making routed support depend on an independently initialized router with a direct, non-monotone routing bonus should produce a genuine architecture signal in proxy and reveal whether separate support selection can outperform the stalled gated search.

Seven non-improving rounds indicate the current gated-centered search is fundamentally stalled, and the updated routed encoder is now the highest-value different architecture to test because it separates support selection from reconstruction magnitude without relying on the same gated monotone ranking. A clean proxy run of routed at K=128 under the best validated training regime can reveal whether this support-selection change opens a new quality path that top-k and gated did not.

Severe stagnation and two non-competitive K=128 architecture probes suggest the current global-ranking support mechanisms are locally exhausted. A new `group_topk` prototype that enforces one local winner per small latent group before the global top-k may reduce redundant feature selection and improve reconstruction quality or cost under the same validated training regime.

After nine non-improving rounds and non-competitive K=128 results from topk and routed, the current global support-ranking direction looks structurally weak. The newly added `group_topk` family is the next fundamentally different architecture probe because it imposes local winner-take-all competition before global selection, which may improve feature diversity and reduce redundant support allocation under the same validated training regime.

In crash recovery mode, the safest informative move is to return to the best known working gated K=128 configuration and make the smallest remaining stabilization adjustment. A milder auxiliary pressure of AUXK_ALPHA=0.01 may preserve the strong gated baseline while avoiding the degradation seen at 0.0625, giving a cleaner read on whether the current anchor is slightly over-regularized.

With the gated family now improved at K=128 via AUXK_ALPHA=0.01, the next clean move is to test whether that stronger regime transfers to a smaller sparsity budget and improves the Pareto frontier rather than only the anchor point. A gated K=64 proxy run under the same stabilized settings can reveal whether the current best family can materially outperform the old JumpReLU K=64 point.
